channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump vows to take back 'stolen' wealth as tariffs on steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump openly challenged U.S. allies on Wednesday by increasing tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25% as he vowed to take back wealth “stolen” by other countries, drawing quick retaliation from Europe and Canada.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI warns of extortion and self-harm networks targeting minors
item.description: FBI Denver said that 764, a decentralized and transnational sextortion network, is seeing an increase in activity, as well as other violent online networks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has signaled that it could narrow which set of waters receive protections under the Clean Water Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government asks that detained Columbia student's legal fight be moved to New Jersey or Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A government lawyer asked a federal judge in Manhattan on Wednesday to move the legal fight over the detention of Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil to New Jersey or Louisiana, two locations where he has been held as the Trump administration seeks to deport him over
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: They crossed the world to reach the US. Now deported under Trump, they're stuck in Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — They crossed oceans to get to the U.S., fleeing conflict, religious persecution, poverty and government crackdowns in countries such as Afghanistan, Somalia, Cameroon, China, Pakistan and Iran.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina House Republicans fight with each other over the budget
item.description: At least three-quarters of South Carolina’s House members approved the more than 100 sections of the state’s $14 billion spending plan this week.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas execution of 'desert killer,' on death row for 30 years, blocked by court
item.description: David Wood, 67, was sentenced to death in 1992 for the killing of six woman and girls in El Paso, Texas. He has spent nearly 33 years on death row for the crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia anti-Israel protest ringleader Mahmoud Khalil faces court hearing on detention
item.description: A Manhattan federal judge will hear a challenge to the detention of Mahmoud Khalil, one of the ringleaders of anti-Israel protests at Columbia University last year who the Trump administration is trying to deport.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing American college student's hometown sheriff names person of interest five days into search
item.description: The Loudoun County Sheriff’s Office in Virginia named Joshua Riibe, 24, as a "person of interest" in the disappearance of University of Pittsburgh student, Sudiksha Konanki.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: The House on Tuesday passed a funding bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up the measure for consideration in the Senate. The chamber cleared the continuing resolution (CR) in a largely party-line 217-213 vote, with just one Democrat — Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing woman survives nearly a week after Northwest Indiana crash
item.description: Brieonna Cassell, 41, of Wheatfield, was reported missing last Thursday, according to the Newton County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas restaurant serving more than just food by helping at-risk young adults
item.description: From the colorful décor to messages of aspirations, Blooming Bistro Co-Owner Samantha Steele wants to serve hope in every bite.
item.dc:creator: Mary Jane Belleza, NewsNation

item.title: How microplastics may be stoking antibiotic resistance
item.description: The microscopic shards of plastic found in every corner of the planet may be exacerbating antibiotic resistance, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: FBI joins search for US college student who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The FBI has joined the search for a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who went missing nearly a week ago while on spring break in the Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was killed inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Popeyes' signature sauces will soon be available at grocery stores nationwide
item.description: For the first time, Popeyes customers can purchase bottles of its popular sauces when they head to retail stores nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: American college student missing in Dominican Republic didn't drown, Natalee Holloway private eye believes
item.description: University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki's disappearance from a Punta Cana resort should be seen as suspicious, according to a private investigator.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get over $25M: What to know about payments
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: Resentencing, which could make the brothers immediately eligible for parole, is just one of the pathways they are pursuing to walk free after their 1996 convictions for murder.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
